concepts_list.name: density
concepts_list.description: Fluid density, appearing in the momentum and mass balance equations
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly denoted as ρ in the balance of linear momentum and mass for incompressible flow.

concepts_list.name: viscosity
concepts_list.description: Dynamic viscosity of the fluid, which may depend on shear rate and is a key parameter in generalised Newtonian fluids
concepts_list.justification: Defined as μ in the stress tensor expression and described as a function of shear rate in the Carreau model.

concepts_list.name: velocity_field
concepts_list.description: Fluid velocity vector field, denoted by u, which is the primary unknown in the momentum equation
concepts_list.justification: Denoted by u in the momentum equation and appears in both convective and stress terms.

concepts_list.name: pressure_field
concepts_list.description: Fluid pressure field, denoted by p, which is coupled with velocity through the incompressibility constraint
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the momentum equation and is subject to boundary conditions on Γ_N.

concepts_list.name: shear_rate
concepts_list.description: Shear rate defined as the square root of half the strain rate tensor, used to model nonlinear viscosity in generalised Newtonian fluids
concepts_list.justification: Defined as γ˙ = √(½ ∇s u : ∇s u) and used in the Carreau model for viscosity dependence.

concepts_list.name: velocity_h
concepts_list.description: Discrete approximation of the velocity field in the finite element space, denoted by u_h
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the weak form as u_h, with subscript h indicating finite element discretisation.

concepts_list.name: pressure_h
concepts_list.description: Discrete approximation of the pressure field in the finite element space, denoted by p_h
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the weak form as p_h, with subscript h indicating finite element discretisation.

concepts_list.name: test_function_w_h
concepts_list.description: Test function for velocity in the weak form, denoted by w_h, used in the variational formulation
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the weak form of the momentum equation with subscript h indicating finite element discretisation.

concepts_list.name: normal_traction_t
concepts_list.description: Normal boundary traction vector, defined as (2μ∇s u − pI)n, used in the Neumann boundary condition
concepts_list.justification: Defined as t in the boundary condition equation and appears as a vector on Γ_N.

concepts_list.name: boundary_gamma_n
concepts_list.description: Neumann boundary part of the domain boundary, where normal tractions are prescribed
concepts_list.justification: Denoted as Γ_N in the boundary conditions and appears in the traction equation on the boundary.

concepts_list.name: strain_rate_tensor
concepts_list.description: Symmetric gradient operator ∇s used to compute the strain rate tensor from the velocity field
concepts_list.justification: Appears as ∇s in multiple equations, including in the definition of shear rate and stress tensor.

Dynamic viscosity of the fluid, which may depend on shear rate and is a key parameter in generalised Newtonian fluids
